headline: JFrog's public DirtyClone exploit hands local Linux users root the same week the Linux Foundation launches Akrites, as AI keeps shrinking the window between vulnerability disclosure and attack.

emerging_trends.text: Software supply-chain attackers keep mutating the Shai-Hulud lineage, with Miasma now seeding malicious npm releases in the LeoPlatform and RStreams packages, abusing GitHub Actions, and crossing into the Go ecosystem.
emerging_trends.topic_title: Miasma Spreads to npm, GitHub Actions, and Go

emerging_trends.text: Enterprises racing to deploy autonomous AI agents face a widening governance gap, prompting Proof's x401 identity protocol and a Model Context Protocol overhaul that pushes security duties onto developers.

AI keeps compressing the gap between disclosure and attack, the premise behind the Linux Foundation's new Akrites framework for faster open-source patching and JFrog's same-week DirtyClone root exploit.

topics.title: AI Agents Strain Identity Governance
topics.latest_developments: Proof launched x401, an issuer-neutral protocol that lets a website or API verify the identity and authorization behind an AI agent, the same day a major Model Context Protocol overhaul shifted critical security responsibilities onto developers and platform operators.
topics.summary: Autonomous AI agents inherit human permissions and act at machine speed with minimal oversight, widening a governance gap that legacy identity infrastructure never anticipated; controls like x401 and guardian-agent layers aim to authenticate and constrain them.

sources.title: Proof's x401 establishes an open protocol for AI agent identity and authorization
Guardian Agents: The Next Layer of Identity Governance
New Enterprise-Ready MCP Specification Brings New Security Challenges

topics.title: Linux Foundation Launches Akrites
topics.latest_developments: The Linux Foundation unveiled Akrites, an industry initiative uniting technology companies, financial institutions, security vendors, and AI firms to coordinate remediation and disclosure of vulnerabilities in widely used open-source software.
topics.summary: Akrites responds to AI shrinking the time between flaw discovery and exploitation by giving critical open-source projects shared tools and channels to report, patch, and disclose vulnerabilities.

sources.title: Critical open-source projects get a new security framework
Linux Foundation Unveils New Open Source Security Project Akrites

topics.title: DirtyClone Linux Kernel Root Exploit
topics.latest_developments: JFrog Security Research published a working exploit walkthrough for DirtyClone on June 25, the first public demonstration of this DirtyFrag-family variant.
topics.summary: DirtyClone, tracked as CVE-2026-43503 with a CVSS score of 8.8, lets a local user corrupt file-backed memory through a cloned network packet and escalate to root on Linux systems; administrators should apply the upstream kernel patch.

sources.title: New DirtyClone Linux Kernel Flaw Lets Local Users Gain Root via Cloned Packets

topics.title: Synology Patches Critical MailPlus Server Flaws
topics.latest_developments: Synology issued a critical fix for three MailPlus Server flaws, led by CVE-2026-13136, a faulty authorization check that lets remote attackers read or write arbitrary files and trigger denial-of-service, alongside CVE-2026-13135.
topics.summary: MailPlus Server runs private email infrastructure on Synology NAS devices; owners should install the security update promptly to block remote file access and DoS conditions.

sources.title: Synology issues critical fix for MailPlus Server vulnerabilities

topics.title: Miasma Spreads to npm, GitHub Actions, and Go
topics.latest_developments: The latest wave delivers malicious npm releases in the LeoPlatform and RStreams packages, abuses GitHub Actions workflows, and propagates into the Go ecosystem.
topics.summary: Miasma belongs to the self-replicating Mini Shai-Hulud and Hades malware family that hijacks developer packages to steal secrets and spread; teams pinning npm and Go dependencies and auditing GitHub Actions workflows can limit exposure.

sources.title: Miasma Malware Targets npm Packages and GitHub Actions in Supply Chain Attack

topics.title: FCC Tightens Undersea Cable Rules
topics.latest_developments: The FCC voted to toughen rules protecting undersea cables and said it plans to mandate licensing for owners and operators of submarine line terminal equipment.
topics.summary: Submarine cables carry most international internet traffic and face espionage and sabotage risk; the FCC's licensing mandate would extend federal oversight to the SLTE gear that lands those cables.

sources.title: FCC votes to toughen rules in bid to better protect undersea cables

weather: Saturday: Showers And Thunderstorms Likely then Slight Chance Showers And Thunderstorms, high 80F.

games.recap: Brandon Lowe, Henry Davis each homer to lead Pirates over Mariners 5-1

business_politics.title: Venezuela Races to Restructure Debt After Quakes
business_politics.latest_developments: Two powerful earthquakes struck northern Venezuela this week, and the U.S.-backed government is now pressing bondholders for a fast debt restructuring even as some creditors urge it to slow down.
business_politics.summary: Back-to-back earthquakes near Caracas pushed Venezuela into a national state of emergency, straining an economy already in sovereign default, and the government now wants a quick deal with bondholders while some warn against rushing a restructuring of the country's tens of billions in defaulted debt.

sources.title: How quickly should a battered Venezuela restructure its debt?
The Night the Ground Wouldn't Stop Shaking in Venezuela

business.title: Ex-Worker Charged in Ventec Refrigeration Theft
business.latest_developments: The Allegheny County District Attorney announced June 26 charges against a former Ventec Refrigeration employee accused of stealing nearly $300,000 from the Penn Hills company.
business.summary: Ashley Apperson, 34, of Leechburg, who worked roughly four years at Ventec Refrigeration in Penn Hills, faces multiple charges after county detectives say she took close to $300,000 from the firm.

sources.title: Woman accused of stealing nearly $300,000 from Penn Hills refrigeration company

business.title: Eco-Soap Bank's Global Reach From Pittsburgh
business.latest_developments: Pittsburgh City Paper profiled June 26 how the Pittsburgh-based Eco-Soap Bank turns manufacturing soap waste into bars for people who lack them worldwide.
business.summary: The Eco-Soap Bank, a Pittsburgh nonprofit, collects scrap and surplus soap from manufacturers, reprocesses it, and distributes bars to communities in need across the globe.

sources.title: From Pittsburgh, the Eco-Soap Bank has used scrap soap to make a global splash

around_town.title: County Commits $18M to Tree of Life Rebuild
around_town.latest_developments: Allegheny County authorized up to $18 million on June 26 toward redeveloping the Tree of Life site in Squirrel Hill into a remembrance and education center.
around_town.summary: Allegheny County approved as much as $18 million to help rebuild the Tree of Life site in Squirrel Hill into a place of remembrance, education, and community programming, the largest public commitment yet to the long-planned project.

sources.title: Allegheny County authorizes up to $18M for Tree of Life redevelopment

around_town.title: Pennsylvania's Missing Fertility-Coverage Mandate
around_town.latest_developments: PublicSource reported June 26 that Pennsylvania, unlike half of U.S. states, requires no insurance coverage for fertility treatment even as the Pittsburgh region courts more young families.
around_town.summary: Pennsylvania mandates no insurance coverage for fertility treatments such as IVF, while 25 states require some, a gap PublicSource links to the state's stagnant population and Pittsburgh's effort to attract families.

sources.title: Pittsburgh wants more families, but state insurance laws make that harder

around_town.title: Commercial Street Closed a Second Day for Bridge Test
around_town.latest_developments: PennDOT closed Commercial Street again on June 26 for a second straight day of testing the equipment that will slide the new span into place, without explaining why the extra day was needed.
around_town.summary: PennDOT crews shut Commercial Street near Frick Park for a second consecutive day to test the system that will move a roughly 22-million-pound replacement bridge span into position next month.

sources.title: Commercial Street closed for second day as PennDOT crews do more testing

around_teams.title: Pirates Celebrate Don Kelly's 100th Win
around_teams.latest_developments: The Post-Gazette chronicled June 26 the Pirates marking manager Don Kelly's 100th career victory, with players and staff praising his leadership.
around_teams.summary: Pirates players and front office celebrated manager Don Kelly reaching 100 career wins, a milestone the Post-Gazette framed around clubhouse respect and hopes for a postseason push.

sources.title: 'He deserves it': The Pirates celebrate Don Kelly's 100th win — and hope more are on the way

around_teams.title: Pirates Open Legacy Hall at PNC Park
around_teams.latest_developments: The Post-Gazette detailed June 25 the Pirates' new Legacy Hall at PNC Park, a history space the team says will let fans 'see a lot of history.'
around_teams.summary: The Pirates are opening Legacy Hall at PNC Park, an exhibit space showcasing franchise and Pittsburgh baseball history for fans young and old.

sources.title: The Pirates' new Legacy Hall at PNC Park will allow fans young and old to 'see a lot of history'

around_teams.title: Dulac Fields Steelers Offseason Questions
around_teams.latest_developments: In his June 24 chat, Post-Gazette beat writer Gerry Dulac took reader questions on cornerback Joey Porter Jr., the quarterback room, and the roster heading toward training camp.
around_teams.summary: Post-Gazette Steelers beat writer Gerry Dulac answered reader questions covering Joey Porter Jr., quarterbacks Aaron Rodgers, Will Howard, and rookie Drew Allar, and Pittsburgh's roster outlook for 2026.

sources.title: Gerry Dulac's Steelers chat transcript: 06.24.26

team_usa.title: Pochettino Defends Group Win After Türkiye Loss
team_usa.latest_developments: After the United States lost 3-2 to Türkiye on June 25, coach Mauricio Pochettino turned combative with reporters, insisting his side still 'won the group,' while backup keeper Matt Turner drew blame for the late collapse.
team_usa.summary: The United States, already through as Group D winners, fell 3-2 to Türkiye on Kaan Ayhan's final-kick goal at SoFi Stadium with a rotated lineup; Pochettino pushed back on 'weird' postgame questions, ESPN graded reserve goalkeeper Matt Turner 3/10, and the U.S. now meets Bosnia and Herzegovina in the round of 32 at Santa Clara on Wednesday, July 1.

sources.title: Poch puzzled by 'weird' questions: We 'won group'
USMNT to face Bosnia in World Cup round of 32

reading.author: Stratechery
reading.title: An Interview with Figma CEO Dylan Field About Design and AI
reading.summary: Field walks through how Figma built its design platform and argues that AI works as a tailwind for the company rather than a threat to it.

reading.author: Ed Zitron
reading.title: Cargo Culture
reading.summary: Zitron argues the AI industry increasingly mimics the surface rituals and trappings of transformative technology while lacking the economics or product substance to justify its valuations.

reading.author: Cal Newport
reading.title: Dear AI Companies: Stop the "Doom Trolling"
reading.summary: Newport criticizes AI firms for publicizing alarming claims about their own products' dangers, likening it to Ford warning its F-150 might be uncontrollable, and casts the practice as a marketing tactic that erodes trust.

sports_media: Not Just Football with Cam Heyward
